title: The kings of world football. Brazil lead the way with five FIFA World ...
content: Here are the top 4 nations with the most FIFA Men's World Cup titles in history! Brazil – 5 Titles Germany – 4 Titles Italy – 4 Titles

title: Teams with the most World Cup titles - FIFA
content: 1st) Brazil – 5 titles 2nd) Germany – 4 titles 2nd) Italy – 4 titles 4th) Argentina – 3 titles 5th) France – 2 titles 5th) Uruguay – 2

title: FIFA World Cup winners list: Know the champions - Olympics.com
content: Brazil have won the FIFA World Cup five times, the most in history, while Germany and Italy have won it four times each. FIFA World Cup 2022 winners Argentina. Argentina are the reigning world champions, having won the **FIFA World Cup 2022** in Qatar. It was also the first FIFA World Cup to be held in the middle of the club football season. Uruguay also went on to lift the trophy at home, beating Argentina 4-2 in the final to be crowned the first FIFA World Cup champions. Uruguay won their second title in 1950 and West Germany lifted their first FIFA World Cup trophy in 1954. Brazil are the most successful team at the FIFA World Cup, having won the title five times. They are also the only country to play in all 22 editions of the FIFA World Cup. Brazil’s first title came in 1958 as the 17-year-old Pele, who would go on to become a legend, scored six goals in the tournament.

title: Brazil remains the most successful men's FIFA World Cup nation ...
content: Brazil has won the World Cup five times, in 1958, 1962, 1970, 1994, and 2002. Italy has won the World Cup four times, in 1934, 1938, 1982, and
